verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-terminal lipid modifications: N-myristoylation at Gly-2 and palmitoylation at Cys-3. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain. Its entire protein body faces the cytosol. Loss-of-function mutants lacking both acylation sites delocalize to the cytoplasm, confirming that lipid anchoring is the sole membrane-association mechanism. No documented outer-leaflet, cancer-ecto, or cell-death-induced surface exposure of the LYN protein body has been reported.
